annotations.label: Subset and superset

anchor.snippet: a set A is a subset of a set B if and only if all elements of A are also elements of B

annotations.note: Set.Subset defines `s ⊆ t` as `∀ ⦃a⦄, a ∈ s → a ∈ t`, exactly the subset relation.

annotations.label: Proper subset

anchor.snippet: if they are unequal, then A is a proper subset of B

annotations.note: Set's `⊂` (HasSSubset, characterized by ssubset_def) is proper subset: `s ⊆ t ∧ ¬t ⊆ s`.

annotations.label: k-subset

anchor.snippet: A k -subset is a subset with k elements.

annotations.note: Set.powersetCard n is the set of finite subsets of cardinality n, i.e. the k-subsets.

annotations.label: Element argument proof technique

anchor.snippet: a proof technique known as the element argument

annotations.note: A proof methodology, not a mathematical statement, so it has no Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Subset / superset (formal)

anchor.snippet: If A and B are sets and every element of A is also an element of B

annotations.note: Set.subset_def states `s ⊆ t` equals `∀ x, x ∈ s → x ∈ t`, the formal definition.

annotations.label: Proper (strict) subset (formal)

anchor.snippet: If A is a subset of B , but A is not equal to B

annotations.note: Set.ssubset_iff_subset_ne states `s ⊂ t ↔ s ⊆ t ∧ s ≠ t`, the strict-subset definition.

annotations.label: Empty set is a subset of any set

anchor.snippet: has no elements, and therefore is vacuously a subset of any set X .

annotations.note: Set.empty_subset proves `∅ ⊆ s` for any set s.

annotations.label: Reflexivity of inclusion

anchor.snippet: Reflexivity : Given any set

annotations.note: Set.Subset.refl proves `a ⊆ a` for any set.

annotations.label: Transitivity of inclusion

anchor.snippet: Transitivity : If

annotations.note: Set.Subset.trans proves `a ⊆ b → b ⊆ c → a ⊆ c`.

annotations.label: Antisymmetry of inclusion

anchor.snippet: Antisymmetry : If

annotations.note: Set.Subset.antisymm proves `a ⊆ b → b ⊆ a → a = b`.

annotations.label: Irreflexivity of proper subset

anchor.snippet: Irreflexivity : Given any set

annotations.note: Set's `⊂` is `<`, so lt_irrefl `¬ a < a` gives irreflexivity as a special case of any preorder.

annotations.label: Transitivity of proper subset

anchor.snippet: Transitivity : If

annotations.note: Set's `⊂` is `<`, so lt_trans gives transitivity of proper subset as a preorder instance.

annotations.label: Asymmetry of proper subset

anchor.snippet: Asymmetry : If

annotations.note: Set's `⊂` is `<`, so lt_asymm `a < b → ¬ b < a` gives asymmetry as a preorder instance.

annotations.label: ⊂ / ⊃ as subset / superset

anchor.snippet: Some authors use the symbols

annotations.note: Mathlib does not adopt the convention of `⊂`/`⊃` meaning non-strict subset/superset (it reserves `⊆`/`⊇`).

annotations.label: ⊂ / ⊃ as proper subset / superset

anchor.snippet: Other authors prefer to use the symbols

annotations.note: Mathlib follows this convention: `⊂` (Set.instHasSSubset) denotes proper subset, defined as `<`.

annotations.label: {1,2} proper subset of {1,2,3}

anchor.snippet: The set A = {1, 2} is a proper subset of B = {1, 2, 3}

annotations.note: An illustrative numeric example with no dedicated Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: {1,2,3} subset but not proper of {1,2,3}

anchor.snippet: The set D = {1, 2, 3} is a subset (but not a proper subset) of E = {1, 2, 3}

annotations.note: An illustrative numeric example with no dedicated Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Primes >10 proper subset of odds >10

anchor.snippet: The set { x : x is a prime number greater than 10} is a proper subset

annotations.note: An illustrative example with no dedicated Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Naturals proper subset of rationals

anchor.snippet: The set of natural numbers is a proper subset of the set of rational numbers

annotations.note: No named declaration states ℕ as a proper subset of ℚ (Mathlib uses coercions/embeddings rather than set inclusion here).

annotations.label: Rationals proper subset of reals

anchor.snippet: The set of rational numbers is a proper subset of the set of real numbers

annotations.note: No named declaration states ℚ as a proper subset of ℝ (Mathlib uses the Rat.cast ring hom rather than set inclusion).

annotations.label: Euler diagram example

anchor.snippet: Another example in an Euler diagram : A is a proper subset of B.

annotations.note: A diagram-based illustrative example with no Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Power set

anchor.snippet: is called its power set

annotations.note: Set.powerset (`𝒫 s`) is defined as `{t | t ⊆ s}`, the set of all subsets.

annotations.label: Inclusion is a partial order on the power set

anchor.snippet: is a partial order on the set

annotations.note: Set α is a BooleanAlgebra whose underlying `≤`/`⊆` is the inclusion partial order, generalizing the partial order on a power set.

annotations.label: Reverse set inclusion partial order

anchor.snippet: We may also partially order

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the reverse of any order via OrderDual; applied to `Set α` this is the reverse set-inclusion partial order.

annotations.label: Power set order isomorphic to Cartesian product

anchor.snippet: the inclusion partial order is—up to an order isomorphism —the Cartesian product

annotations.note: Equiv.Set.powerset gives a bijection `𝒫 S ≃ Set S` but Mathlib has no stated order isomorphism to a Cartesian product of two-element orders.

annotations.label: Notation for k-subsets

anchor.snippet: in analogue with the notation for binomial coefficients

annotations.note: The object (k-subsets) is Set.powersetCard, but the binomial-coefficient-style notation is not introduced in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Subset iff intersection equals A

anchor.snippet: A set A is a subset of B if and only if their intersection is equal to A.

annotations.note: Set.inter_eq_left states `s ∩ t = s ↔ s ⊆ t`.

annotations.label: Subset iff union equals B

anchor.snippet: A set A is a subset of B if and only if their union is equal to B.

annotations.note: Set.union_eq_right states `s ∪ t = t ↔ s ⊆ t`.

annotations.label: Finite subset iff cardinality of intersection equals A

anchor.snippet: A finite set A is a subset of B , if and only if the cardinality of their intersection is equal to the cardinality of A.

annotations.note: Cardinality/subset infrastructure (e.g. Finset.eq_of_subset_of_card_le and Set.inter_eq_left) exists, but the exact `#(A ∩ B) = #A ↔ A ⊆ B` iff is not stated as a single lemma.

annotations.label: Subset relation forms a Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: The subset relation defines a partial order on sets.

annotations.note: Set.instBooleanAlgebra proves `Set α` is a Boolean algebra under inclusion.

annotations.label: Inclusion is the canonical partial order

anchor.snippet: Inclusion is the canonical partial order

annotations.note: The canonical `≤` on `Set α` is inclusion (Set.le_eq_subset), provided by its BooleanAlgebra/partial-order structure.

annotations.label: Ordinals as sets ordered by inclusion

anchor.snippet: The ordinal numbers are a simple example

annotations.note: Mathlib's Ordinal type carries the canonical well-order, with ≤ corresponding (via the von Neumann encoding) to set inclusion of initial segments.
